verses.text: Nadab and Abihu offer 'strange fire,' and fire from the LORD devours them. Strange fire. Worship not commanded is deadly.
Nadab and Abihu offer 'strange fire,' and fire from the LORD devours them. Strange fire. Worship not commanded is deadly.
Moses says, 'I will be sanctified'; the bodies are carried out. Sanctified. God will be honoured in His own.
Moses says, 'I will be sanctified'; the bodies are carried out. Sanctified. God will be honoured in His own.
Aaron and his sons may not mourn or leave, lest they die. No mourning. Duty must not be broken even in grief.
Aaron and his sons may not mourn or leave, lest they die. No mourning. Duty must not be broken even in grief.
Aaron and his sons may not mourn or leave, lest they die. No mourning. Duty must not be broken even in grief.
Priests must not drink wine in the tabernacle, to discern holy and unholy. Sober. Clear judgment is needful in God's service.
Priests must not drink wine in the tabernacle, to discern holy and unholy. Sober. Clear judgment is needful in God's service.
Priests must not drink wine in the tabernacle, to discern holy and unholy. Sober. Clear judgment is needful in God's service.
Priests must not drink wine in the tabernacle, to discern holy and unholy. Sober. Clear judgment is needful in God's service.
The priests are to eat the meat and wave offerings as their portion. The portion. They are fed from the offerings.
The priests are to eat the meat and wave offerings as their portion. The portion. They are fed from the offerings.
The priests are to eat the meat and wave offerings as their portion. The portion. They are fed from the offerings.
The priests are to eat the meat and wave offerings as their portion. The portion. They are fed from the offerings.
Moses is angry the sin offering was not eaten in the holy place. Rebuke. Even Aaron's sons err in the rite.
Moses is angry the sin offering was not eaten in the holy place. Rebuke. Even Aaron's sons err in the rite.
Moses is angry the sin offering was not eaten in the holy place. Rebuke. Even Aaron's sons err in the rite.
Aaron answers gently of his grief, and Moses is content. Mercy. God allows for human weakness in sorrow.
